notes: ONE live Vibe REPL turn: a prose-only multi-sentence assistant reply (Tool calls ≤ 0) that must be a SINGLE contiguous working→ready arc with NO premature flicker to ready before the turn-end signal. VIBE'S EMISSION MODEL (the load-bearing fact): SessionLogger.persist_messages (session_logger.py:240-257) appends each message to messages.jsonl as exactly ONE complete json.dumps(msg)+newline, flush+fsync per message — it never rewrites the last line and never streams partial JSON; incremental tokens are TUI-render only and never reach the file (confirmed against a live 336-line transcript where every line parses as complete JSON). So for a prose turn the whole reply lands as ONE atomic terminal assistant line (role:assistant, non-empty content, no tool_calls) which the parser maps to turn_done (working→ready); the parser has NO assistant_streaming / non-terminal-stop_reason family, and the shared tailer holds any partial trailing bytes until newline (tailer.go:550-562), so no per-flush event can prematurely flip to ready — the Bug-D flicker family the scenario locks down cannot arise on this transport. DOCUMENTED TARGET / no-flicker IDEAL: because the prose reply is one atomic flush, the streaming_window is a single sustained working span rather than many discrete flushes (the same substance-thinness kiro-cli has — but here the daemon HANDLES the transport correctly, so daemon=full, not incapable). This spec asserts the ideal a recording validates: no ready and no waiting across the working span, one clean working→ready settle. DRIVER GAP: the scaffolded driver stubs wait_turn (not_implemented; DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep") and its SEAM 3 transcript-path resolution is a TODO — record ports Vibe's turn-done poll + SEAM 3 before driving. PRESESSION identity: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch until the session_<ts>_<id>/messages.jsonl directory materializes and mints the real session (session_id = the directory name), so session_birth (asserts only 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch') matches the pre-session; turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working (necessarily the real session — the pre-session never enters working), and every later phase pins to it via same_session_as. OBSERVATIONS: model resolves from the sidecar meta.json config.active_model the parser reads (sidecar.go:59-61 → mistral-medium-3.5, unchanged by NormalizeModelName); tokens_nonzero because the parser emits Tokens.Total=stats.context_tokens on turn_done (parser.go:112-114); cost is zero because the adapter's sidecar decoder never reads session_cost (cost unobservable by design). HOW runs differ: exact wording, sub-second timing, and per-turn token count vary (model-output variance); stable structurally — a single sustained working span, no waiting, one working→ready settle, one process_exited, final ready. NOTE: model/tokens are provisional until record confirms them live; single presession→session arc, one process_exited. BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter -- birth re-modeled as presession_birth -> session_birth (new_session, different session_id) templated from the codex sibling; observations now assert agent only (cost & tokens are sidecar-only, unverifiable in the offline replay golden, like kiro-cli), superseding any model/tokens claim above.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the session_<ts>_<id>/messages.jsonl directory appears, born ready
PID bound to the live vibe session (DiscoverPID matches the Python vibe process by command line + cwd)
Prose-only prompt -> working on the real session

During the streamed prose reply the session stays solidly in working -- no flush may promote to ready and state never enters waiting. (DOCUMENTED TARGET: Vibe lands the whole reply as ONE atomic complete assistant line, so today this is a single-flush working span; this asserts the no-flicker ideal a recording validates.)
Only the terminal text-only assistant message (turn_done) settles working -> ready; despite the streamed reply the turn is one contiguous arc and state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the streamed turn -> a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
